title: Mesh Node Automation CLI and Validation Pipeline
description: Extend the automation CLI and API server with mesh-specific commands for validating node development work. Modeled after the Blender CLI validation pattern (see /home/acw/Code/Blender-Procedural-Human/tools/commands/validation.py and geometry.py). The validation pipeline is: launch scene → health check → load node graph JSON → validate geometry → screenshot → shutdown. This ticket adds: (1) new AutomationApiServer endpoints for mesh state, graph loading, and geometry validation, (2) new Python CLI commands (mesh-state, mesh-load-graph, mesh-validate, mesh-probe), (3) a validation orchestrator that runs the full pipeline in sequence. Every subsequent node ticket should validate its work through these commands.
definition-of-done: 1. AutomationApiServer has new routes: GET /mesh/state, POST /mesh/load-graph, GET /mesh/validate. 2. /mesh/state returns JSON with vertexCount, faceCount, edgeCount, eulerCharacteristic, boundingBoxMin, boundingBoxMax, isWatertight. 3. /mesh/load-graph accepts a node graph JSON body, evaluates it, and replaces the current mesh in the viewer. 4. /mesh/validate runs geometry checks (vertex count > 0, face count > 0, not degenerate/collapsed to origin, normals computed) and returns pass/fail with details. 5. CLI commands exist: 'mesh-state', 'mesh-load-graph --file <path>', 'mesh-validate', 'mesh-probe' (health + state + validate + screenshot in one). 6. mesh-probe returns a single JSON with all validation results. 7. Validation commands are documented in context.md.
testing-plan: 1. Launch mesh-viewer scene, run 'mesh-state', verify vertex/face counts for the default cube. 2. Create a simple 2-node graph JSON (MeshCube -> output), run 'mesh-load-graph --file test.json', verify mesh changes in viewport and mesh-state reports new counts. 3. Run 'mesh-validate' on a valid mesh, verify all checks pass. 4. Load a degenerate mesh (all vertices at origin), run 'mesh-validate', verify failure reported. 5. Run 'mesh-probe', verify combined JSON output. 6. Run 'shutdown', verify clean exit.
todos: Add /mesh/state endpoint to AutomationApiServer returning mesh geometry stats
Add /mesh/load-graph endpoint accepting JSON node graph, evaluating it, updating viewer mesh
Add /mesh/validate endpoint running geometry checks (non-empty, not degenerate, normals exist, optionally watertight)
Extend AutomationRuntime.uiState() with mesh section when MeshNodeViewerScene is active
Add 'mesh-validate' CLI command returning pass/fail + details
Add 'mesh-probe' CLI command (health + mesh-state + validate + screenshot in one call)
Create a sample node graph JSON for testing
Update context.md validation section with the new commands
unknowns: Whether /mesh/load-graph should accept inline JSON or only file paths
Whether mesh-validate should include watertight check by default or as an opt-in flag
Whether to add a 'mesh-list-nodes' command that lists all registered MeshNode types from the registry
